svg.text: To compute BIF(dog, cat) at one checkpoint:
From the 240 SGLD posterior samples, extract two columns of losses:

g.text: For each posterior sample: multiply the two deviations from mean, then average
When dog loss is high, cat loss
tends to be high too — they
When dog loss is high, cat loss
tends to be low — they want
Negate so the sign matches the influence convention
Negative BIF: upweighting dog lowers cat's expected loss
Positive BIF: upweighting dog raises cat's expected loss
Do this for all 8 x 8 pairs → full influence matrix at this checkpoint

svg.text: Repeat the entire procedure at each checkpoint → influence traces over training
